decisions.decision: Treat the already-landed service-index and baseline `/v1/accounts/{id}/positions` assertions as the starting point, and extend coverage in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` instead of duplicating the route in threaded HTTP or integration classes for this step.
alternatives_rejected: Re-add route or service-index coverage that is already present in the current checkout.
Mirror the new regressions across concurrency and integration classes before the unit/projection layer is complete.

decisions.decision: Keep `/positions` regressions strict on the public contract by asserting exact ordered `routes.accounts` and exact ordered `account.positions` rows while treating `meta` as dynamic, and add a focused replay-order regression for deterministic `(filledAt, id)` chronology because chronology affects P&L outcomes independently of wire ordering.
alternatives_rejected: Use loose membership assertions for service-index or positions payloads.
Test only lexical wire ordering and leave replay chronology implicit.

decisions.decision: Mirror `/exposure` projection-local lifecycle semantics for `/positions`, so missing accounts, risk-only or probability-edit-only accounts with no live `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` document, and fully pruned accounts all stay `404 account_not_found`.
alternatives_rejected: Return `200` with an empty `positions` list for risk-only or fully pruned accounts.
Differentiate missing, risk-only, and pruned accounts with separate status codes.

decisions.decision: Add representative P&L regressions by patching `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(...)` to fixed marks and asserting exact `unrealizedPnl` for multi-fill accumulation, partial close survivors, and sign-flip survivors, while acknowledging those assertions require the step-3 replay/P&L production seam to be present.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only relative P&L behavior against live inference outputs.
Delay all exact P&L coverage until a later integration-only sweep.

decisions.decision: Prefer accepted EventTrade writes for realistic accumulation and offset scenarios, and reserve direct `ORDERS` or `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` seeding for fixtures that the public write path cannot express cleanly, especially deterministic replay-order and missing-history or pruned-account cases.
alternatives_rejected: Force every regression through public POSTs even when the setup becomes opaque.
Seed all cases directly and give up black-box route realism.

steps.task: Audit the existing `/positions` assertions in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` and update them to remain the canonical baseline for exact service-index ordering, exact ordered success payloads, and dynamic `meta` handling once `unrealizedPnl` is available on the wire.

Extend projection-level `/positions` negative coverage to pin `404 account_not_found` for missing accounts, risk-only or probability-edit-only accounts with no live `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` document, and fully pruned accounts whose surviving rows round to zero or serialize away.

Add focused deterministic-order regressions around the positions read path, using direct `ORDERS` and `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` seeding where needed to prove stable replay order by `(filledAt, id)` and stable wire order by lexical `(marketId, outcomeId)`.

Add representative `/positions` P&L regressions built mostly from accepted EventTrade writes, patching `query_market_atomic_probability_for_inference(...)` to fixed marks and asserting exact `unrealizedPnl` for multi-fill weighted-average accumulation, partial close survivors, and sign-flip survivors.

Run the targeted `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` slice covering the touched `/positions` regressions, and if the current branch still lacks the step-3 replay/P&L production seam, land or rebase onto that prerequisite before expecting the new exact P&L assertions to pass.
